title: Crops and Random Resizing
concept: Random cropping cuts out a sub-region of an image, while random resizing changes its scale. Together, they teach a CNN that an object can be located anywhere in the frame and appear at any distance from the camera.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Why is horizontal flipping unsafe for handwritten digit datasets?

options.text: It turns numbers like 3 into invalid backwards symbols
It makes the background black and white
It resizes the numbers so they are too small to see

exercises.explain: Yes! Flipping digits or letters ruins their meaning.

cards.title: Always in the Center?
cards.body: If every training photo features a dog perfectly centered and filling the frame, the model might fail when a user uploads a photo where the dog is small and off to the left. We need to teach the model that position and size don't change what an object is.

cards.title: Random Crops & Resizing
cards.body: Random cropping cuts out a rectangular portion of the image, discarding the rest. Random resizing scales the image up or down. Often combined as 'Random Resized Crop', where we take a crop of random size/ratio and resize it back to the target model input size.

cards.title: Looking through a cardboard tube
cards.body: Imagine looking at a room through a cardboard tube. As you move the tube around, you only see patches of the room (crops). You still recognize the couch whether you see a close-up of a cushion or a distant view of the whole frame. That is what random cropping teaches a CNN.

cards.title: Detecting Cars
cards.body: For a self-driving car model, random cropping simulates a car being partially cut off by the edge of the camera frame, while resizing simulates cars being near (large) or far away (small). Both are critical for real safety.

cards.title: Don't crop out the subject entirely
cards.body: If your crop is too aggressive (e.g. taking a tiny 10% patch of a 1000x1000 image), you might crop out the dog completely and leave only a patch of grass. The label is still 'dog', but the model only sees grass! Most pipelines keep crops above 50-80% of the original image area.

cards.title: Safe vs. Aggressive Cropping
cards.body: Varying the crop bounding box size:

left.text:
Keeps the main object in view
Simulates offset positioning
Teases the model with context

right.text:
Risks losing the object entirely
Model sees blank background
Confuses the model with wrong labels

exercises.prompt: What does random cropping teach a CNN model?

options.text: That the object of interest can appear anywhere in the frame, even partially cut off
How to convert color images to grayscale
To ignore the foreground and focus only on the background
How to compress the dataset into a ZIP file

exercises.explain: Correct! Cropping teaches position invariance and robustness to partial occlusion.

exercises.prompt: True or false: An extremely aggressive crop (like 5% of the image) is always better because it forces the model to look closer.
exercises.explain: False. Extreme crops risk removing the labeled object entirely, training the model on background noise with a misleading label.

exercises.sentence: Random ___ simulates objects being closer or farther from the camera during training.
exercises.explain: Resizing changes the scale, helping the model learn scale invariance (recognizing near vs. far objects).

exercises.prompt: Which combination is a standard industry preprocessing technique for training CNNs on varying image sizes?

options.text: Random Resized Crop, which takes a random crop and resizes it to a fixed size
Permanently deleting the borders of all images on disk before training
Converting every image to a vector shape mathematically

exercises.explain: Correct! Random Resized Crop is widely used to handle shape/scale variations during training.

exercises.prompt: You are training a model to identify bird species. If your training dataset only contains photos of birds sitting exactly centered on branches, how will random cropping help?

options.text: By shifting the birds to the corners/edges of the crop, it prepares the model to recognize birds that appear off-center in real-world deployment
It will speed up training by 50% because the cropped images are smaller
It will change the color of the birds' feathers to create new synthetic species
It will not help at all because models can only recognize centered objects

exercises.explain: Perfect. Cropping simulates off-center objects, breaking the 'always centered' bias. Next, let's look at translations and shearing!
